evals.name: no-silent-swallow
evals.expected_output: The agent narrows the catch so only the expected CacheMiss is treated as a normal cold-cache None, while ConnectionError (and any other unexpected failure) surfaces instead of being swallowed. It does not leave a bare except-Exception-return-None that hides the connection failure.
assertions: Catches CacheMiss specifically rather than a bare `except Exception`
A cold cache still returns None (or triggers a recompute), preserving the intended behavior
A ConnectionError is no longer swallowed: it propagates, or is logged with its detail and re-raised, rather than silently becoming None
If any broad catch remains, it logs the full error rather than discarding it
Explains that the original code hid the connection failure behind the same None as a normal miss

evals.name: preserve-cause-and-context
evals.expected_output: The agent chains the original exception with `raise ... from e` so the cause and traceback survive, and enriches the message with the path (and ideally narrows the caught types to OSError/YAMLError). It does not keep flattening every failure into a causeless generic string.
assertions: Uses `raise ConfigError(...) from e` (or equivalent) so the underlying cause and its traceback are preserved
The new message names the config path, so the reader can act on it
Does not reduce the original exception to a bare string that discards the exception object and its type
Explains that the original discarded the cause, so the caller could not tell a missing file from malformed YAML

evals.name: fail-early-not-plausible
evals.expected_output: The agent makes an unknown currency fail loudly at the point of the bad input rather than silently multiplying by a 0 rate and returning a plausible-but-wrong 0. The error names the offending currency.
assertions: An unknown currency raises an error instead of defaulting the rate to 0 and returning 0 cents
The error message includes the offending currency value so the caller can locate it
Does not 'robustify' by catching and returning a fallback total; it fails at the bad input
Explains that the silent 0-rate default produced a wrong answer with no crash, which is why the bugs were hard to trace

evals.name: no-leak-to-user
evals.expected_output: The agent stops returning the raw exception string to the client and instead logs the full error (with stack) server-side while returning a generic user-facing message tied to a reference id. It does not simply reword the leaked string.
assertions: The raw exception text (str(e)) is no longer returned in the HTTP response body
The full error including the stack trace is logged server-side (e.g. log.exception)
The user-facing response is a generic message, ideally carrying a reference id that ties it to the logged record
Explains the risk: raw exception text can leak SQL, file paths, or connection details to whoever hits the endpoint
